response: TEMPERATURE SHOULD BE CHECKED REGULARLY TO ENSURE SAFE CONDITIONS. STABLE SYSTEMS REQUIRE SUSTAINABLE SUPPORT STRUCTURES. STRESS CAN SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECT SYSTEM STABILITY. SEVERAL STUDIES SHOW SUSTAINED STRESS REDUCES SYSTEMS' STRENGTH. SYSTEMS SHOULD STAY STRONG TO SURVIVE STORMS. STORMS STRIKE SOMETIMES WITHOUT WARNING. STRESS TESTS SHOULD SIMULATE SUCH STRESS. STRESS MAY SLOW SYSTEMS' SPEED AND STABILITY. SYSTEMS MUST STAND STRONG TO SURVIVE STORMS. STRONG SYSTEMS SUSTAIN STRESS WITHOUT STOPPING. SYSTEMS SHOULD STAY STRONG AND STABLE. ROLL OUT SYSTEMS THAT STAND STRONG. STRESS AND STRAIN SHOULD STAY WITHIN SAFE LIMITS. SYSTEMS THAT STAND STRONG SURVIVE STORMS. STAYING STRONG IS THE SYSTEM'S STRENGTH.
